features.summary_en: Run a configured shell script immediately before a specific tool executes to validate, log, or block the invocation.
features.description_en: Runs a configured shell script just before a tool is executed. Use the matcher field to target specific tools such as Bash or Edit. Common use cases include blocking dangerous commands like rm -rf before they are carried out, running pre-validation checks on proposed file changes to verify correctness, or logging every tool invocation to an audit trail. The hook can return a block signal with an explanatory message to prevent the targeted tool from running. If multiple PreToolUse hooks match the same tool, they are executed in the order they appear in settings.json, with each hook able to block or modify the invocation.

features.name: PostToolUse Hook
features.summary_ja: ツール実行後のフック
features.description_ja: ツール実行直後に走るフック。ログ記録、自動フォーマット、テスト実行などに使う。
features.summary_en: Run a configured shell script immediately after a specific tool executes for automatic code formatting, testing, or logging.
features.description_en: Runs a configured shell script immediately after a tool finishes executing. Use the matcher field to target specific tools such as Edit or Write. Common use cases include automatically running code formatters like Prettier after file edits, triggering test suites after code changes to catch regressions immediately, and logging tool outputs to a structured audit file. The hook receives the tool's output as input so it can intelligently react to what was produced. PostToolUse hooks that run formatters or linters should be fast, since the main session waits for the hook to complete before continuing.

features.name: Stop Hook
features.summary_ja: Claude応答完了時のフック
features.description_ja: Claudeの応答が終了したときに走るフック。完了通知やセッションログ出力に。
features.summary_en: Run a configured shell script each time Claude finishes generating a response, for completion notifications and session logging.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script each time Claude finishes generating a response and stops producing output. Use this for completion notifications so you know when Claude is done with a long task, for writing session logs after each response, or for any post-response cleanup action. A common example is playing a notification sound or sending a desktop system notification so you can step away from the computer during long tasks and be alerted when Claude needs your attention. The Stop hook receives the full final response as JSON input, making it possible to conditionally trigger different actions based on the content of Claude's answer.

features.name: SubagentStop Hook
features.summary_ja: サブエージェント完了時のフック
features.description_ja: サブエージェント（Task tool）が完了したときに走るフック。
features.summary_en: Run a shell script when a Task-tool sub-agent completes its work, for logging results or triggering downstream processes.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script when a sub-agent launched via the Task tool completes its execution and returns control to the main session. Use this to log sub-agent results to a file for later review, trigger downstream processing pipelines that depend on the sub-agent's output, or send targeted notifications when specific parallel work items finish. Complements the Stop hook which fires for the main agent; this hook fires specifically and only for sub-agents. Identifying which sub-agent triggered the hook is possible through the structured JSON payload passed to the hook script, which includes the agent's ID and type.

features.name: SessionStart Hook
features.summary_ja: セッション開始時のフック
features.description_ja: Claude Code セッション開始時に走るフック。git pull や初期メッセージ表示に。
features.summary_en: Run a configured shell script each time a Claude Code session starts, such as running git pull or displaying a greeting.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script each time a new Claude Code session is created and starts. Common use cases include running git pull to ensure the working tree is synchronized with the latest remote changes before work begins, displaying an initial status message or team announcement, or loading environment-specific configuration. Executes once per session creation, not on session resume. Pairs with SessionEnd for symmetric setup and teardown automation. SessionStart hooks have a short timeout, so keep the script fast and avoid long-running operations that would delay the session from becoming interactive.

features.name: SessionEnd Hook
features.summary_ja: セッション終了時のフック
features.description_ja: セッション終了時に走るフック。コミット忘れ警告、サマリー出力など。
features.summary_en: Run a configured shell script each time a session ends, such as checking uncommitted changes or writing a session summary.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script each time a Claude Code session ends. Common use cases include checking for and warning about uncommitted git changes that might be lost, writing a session summary to a log file for the team's record, or triggering cleanup scripts that should run after every work session. Pairs naturally with SessionStart for symmetric setup and teardown automation across the full session lifecycle. SessionEnd hooks run even if the session is force-quit rather than exited cleanly, providing a reliable cleanup mechanism in most cases.

features.name: UserPromptSubmit Hook
features.summary_ja: ユーザー入力送信時のフック
features.description_ja: ユーザーがプロンプトを送信した直後に走る。入力内容を加工・追記したり、追加コンテキスト注入に使える。

examples.title: Auto-add translation hint
examples.code: UserPromptSubmit: 日本語入力に英訳ヒント追加

features.summary_en: Run a shell script when the user submits a prompt to transform the input or automatically inject additional runtime context.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script immediately after the user submits a prompt and before Claude begins processing it. The hook can append additional instructions to the prompt, translate the input language, or inject dynamic runtime context such as the current git status, environment information, or relevant file contents. Use this to automatically enrich every prompt with project-specific information or to enforce a consistent prompt format and style across all user inputs in the session. The modified prompt returned by the hook replaces the original for Claude's processing, but the original user input is still recorded in the transcript for transparency.

features.name: PreCompact Hook
features.summary_ja: コンテキスト圧縮直前のフック
features.description_ja: コンテキスト圧縮が走る直前に呼ばれる。圧縮前に重要な情報を保存したい時に。
features.summary_en: Run a shell script just before context compression to preserve critical session state before it is summarized and compacted.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script just before context compression is triggered — either manually via /compact or automatically by the system when the context window fills up. Use this to save important information before it gets summarized and potentially obscured, such as dumping the current task list, writing critical session state to a file, or snapshotting important code snippets. Ensures that essential data survives the compaction process and remains accessible after the context is compressed. The hook receives metadata about the current session including token counts and session ID, which can be used to conditionally skip saving for short sessions.

features.name: Notification Hook
features.summary_ja: 通知イベントのフック
features.description_ja: Claudeが通知を出すタイミング（権限要求等）で走るフック。デスクトップ通知連携などに。
features.summary_en: Run a shell script when Claude emits a notification event such as a permission request, rate limit, or task completion.
features.description_en: Runs a configured hook script when Claude emits a notification event, such as requesting user permission for a sensitive operation, hitting a rate limit, or completing a long-running background task. Use this to integrate Claude Code with native desktop notification systems for true asynchronous work. A common example is triggering a macOS system notification via osascript so you can step away from the computer and be alerted without constantly monitoring the terminal. Notification hooks are triggered before the notification is displayed to the user, so the hook can also be used to suppress or redirect certain notification types.

features.name: Plan Mode
features.summary_ja: 読み取り専用の計画モード
features.description_ja: Shift+Tabで切替。コードを書かずに「これからやること」を提案するだけ。実装前のレビューに最適。
features.summary_en: A read-only planning mode where Claude outlines what it would do without making any changes; toggle it with Shift+Tab.
features.description_en: Toggle with Shift+Tab to enter a read-only planning mode where Claude only proposes the steps it would take — reading files, outlining changes, and describing its approach — without actually writing code or modifying the filesystem. Ideal for reviewing Claude's intended implementation plan before committing to it. Use this at the start of a complex task to catch architectural misunderstandings or scope disagreements early, before any actual changes are made that would need to be reversed. While in plan mode, Claude can still read files and search the codebase, but any write or shell-execution requests are rejected with a clear explanation.

features.name: Permission Modes
features.summary_ja: ツール実行時の確認制御
features.description_ja: default(都度確認)、acceptEdits(編集自動)、bypassPermissions(全自動)、plan(読取のみ)の4モード。
features.summary_en: Select from four tool confirmation modes — default, acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, or plan — to balance safety and productivity.
features.description_en: Four modes control how Claude handles tool execution confirmation: default requires explicit confirmation for each tool call, acceptEdits automatically approves all file edits without prompting, bypassPermissions runs all tools fully automatically without any confirmation, and plan allows only read-only operations. Choose the mode that best balances safety and productivity for your current task and risk tolerance. Use acceptEdits for rapid iteration and default for production-sensitive changes. Use bypassPermissions with caution and only in trusted environments, as it removes all safeguards and allows Claude to modify or delete files without confirmation.

features.name: Output Styles
features.summary_ja: 応答スタイルのカスタマイズ
features.description_ja: Claudeの応答口調・形式を調整。簡潔モード、教育モードなどプリセットあり。
features.summary_en: Customize Claude's response tone and format using built-in presets such as concise mode or educational mode for different needs.
features.description_en: Adjusts Claude's response tone, verbosity, and format to match your working style. Available presets include concise mode for brief, direct answers optimized for rapid iteration, and educational mode for detailed explanations with background context suitable for learning. Use this to tailor how Claude communicates to fit the situation — terse answers during intensive debugging, or thorough explanations when onboarding a new team member to the codebase. Output style preferences can also be set in CLAUDE.md to apply a specific style automatically for all sessions within a project.

features.name: Headless Mode (--print)
features.summary_ja: 対話なしで一発実行
features.description_ja: --print フラグで対話UIなしに1回だけプロンプトを実行して結果を返す。CIやスクリプト統合に。
features.summary_en: Run a single prompt non-interactively with --print and print the result to stdout for use in CI pipelines and scripts.
features.description_en: The --print flag runs a single prompt without launching the interactive TUI and outputs the result directly to stdout, making it easy to capture in scripts or pipe to other commands. Ideal for integrating Claude into CI pipelines, Makefiles, shell scripts, and any automated workflow where interactive use is not feasible. Most slash commands and tool calls work in headless mode. Combine with --model, --add-dir, and other CLI flags for complete scripting flexibility. The exit code of the headless command reflects whether Claude completed the task successfully, enabling proper error handling in CI pipelines.

features.name: CLAUDE.md
features.summary_ja: プロジェクトガイダンスファイル
features.description_ja: リポジトリ直下に置くと Claude が自動的に読み込むプロジェクトガイダンス。コーディング規約、ディレクトリ構造、注意事項などを書く。
features.summary_en: A project guidance file placed at the repository root that Claude automatically reads at every session start as standing instructions.
features.description_en: Place this file at the repository root and Claude will automatically read it at the start of every session, treating its contents as standing instructions that apply to all work in the project. Use it to document coding conventions, describe the directory structure, list important build commands, and note any project-specific caveats Claude should always be aware of. Generate an initial template with /init, then customize it collaboratively with your team to reflect your actual workflows and standards. Claude respects a hierarchy of CLAUDE.md files: repo root, parent directories, and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md are all read and merged in order of precedence.

features.name: AGENTS.md
features.summary_ja: エージェント定義ファイル
features.description_ja: サブエージェントの挙動を定義。CLAUDE.md と並列で機能する場合あり。

examples.title: Place in project root
examples.code: プロジェクトルートに配置

features.summary_en: A project-level file that defines default sub-agent behavior and tool access, functioning alongside CLAUDE.md in compatible configurations.
features.description_en: Defines the default behavior, tool access, and behavioral constraints for sub-agents launched within the project. In compatible configurations it functions alongside CLAUDE.md, providing agent-specific guidance that is separate from the main Claude session's instructions. Place it at the project root to ensure sub-agents receive consistent, project-appropriate instructions whenever they are launched. Useful for teams that rely heavily on parallel agent workflows and need predictable sub-agent behavior. If AGENTS.md conflicts with CLAUDE.md instructions, CLAUDE.md takes precedence for the main session, while AGENTS.md takes precedence for sub-agent behavior.

features.name: ONBOARDING.md
features.summary_ja: 新規参加者向けガイド
features.description_ja: プロジェクトに初めて触れる人のためのオンボーディング情報。Claude が読み込んでアシストに使う。

examples.title: Place in project root
examples.code: プロジェクトルートに配置

features.summary_en: An onboarding guide for new project contributors that Claude reads automatically and uses to proactively assist new team members.
features.description_en: Contains onboarding information tailored specifically for people who are new to the project. Claude reads this file and uses its content to proactively assist new contributors with common setup questions, workflow guidance, and project conventions without them needing to ask. Place it at the project root alongside CLAUDE.md for automatic pickup. Use /team-onboarding to auto-generate initial content from historical usage patterns, then refine it manually. The onboarding file is read alongside CLAUDE.md, so you can keep project conventions in CLAUDE.md and new-contributor guidance in ONBOARDING.md separately.

features.name: settings.json
features.summary_ja: ユーザー全体の設定ファイル
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/settings.json でグローバル設定。テーマ、フック、許可リスト、環境変数など。
features.summary_en: The global user settings file at ~/.claude/settings.json covering themes, hooks, permission allowlists, and environment variable definitions.
features.description_en: The global configuration file located at ~/.claude/settings.json. Contains user-level settings covering theme selection, hook script definitions, permission allowlists, environment variable definitions, and other global preferences. Settings here apply consistently across all Claude Code sessions for every project. Use /config as a convenient interface for common adjustments, and edit this file directly for advanced configuration such as complex hook scripts or detailed allowlist patterns. The settings file supports environment-variable interpolation in hook script paths, making it easier to write portable configurations that work across different machines.

features.name: settings.local.json
features.summary_ja: プロジェクト固有設定
features.description_ja: .claude/settings.local.json でプロジェクト単位設定。git管理外、個人の許可設定などに。
features.summary_en: A project-specific settings file at .claude/settings.local.json that is excluded from version control for personal configuration overrides.
features.description_en: Project-level configuration stored in .claude/settings.local.json that is automatically excluded from git version control. This makes it appropriate for personal permission rules, local file path overrides, developer-specific environment variables, and other settings that should not be shared with the rest of the team. Works alongside the shared .claude/settings.json to give individuals project-local configuration that does not conflict with team-wide settings. Even though settings.local.json is gitignored, it is still loaded and merged with settings.json, with local values taking precedence over global ones.

features.name: .mcp.json
features.summary_ja: MCPサーバー設定ファイル
features.description_ja: プロジェクトに紐づく MCP サーバーを定義。stdio/SSE/HTTP の各種MCPサーバーを登録できる。
features.summary_en: The project-level MCP server configuration file at .mcp.json for registering stdio, SSE, and HTTP transport server connections.
features.description_en: Defines the MCP servers associated with the project in a .mcp.json file at the project root. Supports three server types: stdio (spawned child process), SSE (HTTP streaming), and HTTP (REST request-response). Claude automatically connects to all configured MCP servers when a new session starts in the project directory, making their tools available without any manual setup steps. Each server entry specifies the type, connection details, and authentication configuration. MCP server connection errors at session start are reported in the /mcp status view and can be diagnosed in more detail with the /doctor command.

features.name: Memory System
features.summary_ja: 永続メモリシステム
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ にユーザー嗜好・プロジェクト情報を蓄積。MEMORY.md がインデックス。
features.summary_en: The persistent cross-session memory store at ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ with MEMORY.md as the directory index file.
features.description_en: Stores user preferences and accumulated project knowledge persistently across sessions in the ~/.claude/projects/<hash>/memory/ directory. The MEMORY.md file serves as the index and primary entry point for the memory system. Claude automatically references stored memories in future interactions, reducing the need to re-explain context each session. Manage memory contents — reviewing, updating, or deleting entries — using the /memory command. The memory system uses a hierarchical structure where MEMORY.md indexes other memory files, allowing large amounts of information to be organized and retrieved efficiently.

features.name: @-mentions
features.summary_ja: ファイルを@で参照
features.description_ja: プロンプト内で @path/to/file と書くと、そのファイルが自動的にコンテキストに追加される。
features.summary_en: Reference any file by typing @path/to/file in a prompt to automatically add the file's full contents to the context.
features.description_en: Typing @path/to/file in a prompt automatically adds the complete contents of that file to the conversation context before Claude processes the request. Use this to quickly provide Claude with relevant source files, configuration files, or documentation without needing to use a Read tool call first. Tab completion for file paths is supported in most terminal environments, making it fast to reference deeply nested files without typing the full path manually. The @ mention is evaluated at the time the prompt is sent, so the file contents added to context always reflect the current state of the file on disk.

features.name: --continue (-c)
features.summary_ja: 前回のセッションを継続
features.description_ja: claude --continue で直前のセッションをそのまま再開。コンテキストを失わずに続けられる。
features.summary_en: Resume the most recent session immediately with --continue or -c, restoring all conversation history and context without a picker.
features.description_en: Running claude --continue or claude -c resumes the most recently active session exactly where it left off, restoring the complete conversation history and all associated context without any session selection step. Use this as the fastest way to return to your current work after closing the terminal or opening a new shell window. No session picker is shown — Claude jumps directly into the previous session immediately. If the most recent session was a very long one, consider using /compact early in the continued session to avoid quickly hitting the context limit again.

features.name: --resume (-r)
features.summary_ja: 特定セッションを再開
features.description_ja: claude --resume <session-id> で過去の任意セッションを再開。
features.summary_en: Resume any specific past session by its ID with --resume (-r), or open an interactive picker to select from all sessions.
features.description_en: Running claude --resume <session-id> resumes a specific past session using its unique identifier. Running claude -r without an ID opens an interactive session picker where you can browse all archived sessions, sorted by recency, and select the one you want to resume. Use this when you need to return to a session that is not the most recent one, or when you have named sessions with /rename and want to find them by name. The session picker shows sessions sorted by their last access time, with the most recently used sessions at the top for quick access.

features.name: --print (-p)
features.summary_ja: ヘッドレスモードで一発実行
features.description_ja: 対話UIを起動せずプロンプトを実行して結果を出力。スクリプト連携に。
features.summary_en: Execute a single prompt non-interactively with --print (-p) and output the result to stdout for shell scripting and CI.
features.description_en: Runs a single prompt without launching the interactive TUI and prints the result to standard output, making it easy to capture in shell variables, pipe to other commands, or integrate into CI/CD pipelines. Useful for generating dynamic content in shell scripts, running Claude as a step in a Makefile, or scripting repetitive code generation tasks. Functionally equivalent to the Headless Mode described in the documentation. Combine with --model for consistent model selection. Standard error from tool calls is captured separately and not included in the main stdout output, making it clean to pipe Claude's responses to downstream tools.

features.name: --model
features.summary_ja: 使用モデルを起動時に指定
features.description_ja: claude --model claude-opus-4-7 のように、起動時に使用モデルを固定。
features.summary_en: Pin the AI model to use at launch time with --model to ensure a specific Claude version is used for the entire session.
features.description_en: Specifies the Claude model to use when launching a new session, for example claude --model claude-opus-4-7. The specified model is used for the entire session unless you explicitly change it mid-session using /model. Use this in shell aliases, npm scripts, or CI configurations to consistently pin a specific model for reproducibility, or to always use a faster or more cost-effective model for automated tasks. If the specified model is not available for your current plan, Claude Code will report an error and fall back to the default model for the session.

features.name: --add-dir
features.summary_ja: 追加の作業ディレクトリを指定
features.description_ja: Claude のアクセスを通常のcwd以外にも広げる。複数リポジトリを横断する作業に。
features.summary_en: Extend Claude's file access to additional directories at session launch with --add-dir for cross-repo and monorepo work.
features.description_en: Extends Claude's file access at session launch to include one or more additional directories beyond the current working directory. Use this when the code relevant to a task is spread across multiple repositories, or when working with a monorepo layout where important packages or shared libraries are in sibling directories. Equivalent to using /add-dir inside an active session but applied at startup time for immediate availability throughout the session. Multiple --add-dir flags can be specified in a single launch command to add several additional directories at once.

features.name: Subagent System
features.summary_ja: サブエージェントの仕組み
features.description_ja: メインのClaudeとは別の文脈で動く専門エージェント。Task tool 経由で起動。複雑タスクの並列処理や文脈分離に。
features.summary_en: The sub-agent system launches specialized agents in isolated contexts for parallel task execution or context-separated delegated work.
features.description_en: Sub-agents are specialized agents that run in execution contexts fully separate from the main Claude session. They are launched via the Task tool and can execute concurrently with the main agent or with other sub-agents. The isolation ensures that sub-agent work does not pollute the main session's context window. Use sub-agents for parallel task execution, strict context isolation, or delegating specialized work such as broad code search, isolated implementation experiments, or concurrent analysis of multiple codebases. The main session always retains control of the overall task; sub-agents are workers that report back, not independent decision-makers.

features.name: Explore Agent
features.summary_ja: 高速読み取り検索エージェント
features.description_ja: コードを探すための読み取り専用検索エージェント。パターン・キーワード・シンボル検索に最適。並列実行で本体の文脈を消費しない。
features.summary_en: A read-only search sub-agent optimized for fast pattern, keyword, and symbol discovery without consuming the main session context.
features.description_en: A read-only agent optimized specifically for searching and discovering code across the repository. Best suited for wildcard pattern matching, keyword searches, symbol lookups, and identifying all usages of a particular function or variable. Runs in parallel with the main session without consuming its context window, leaving more room for the main agent's conversation and reasoning. Use this to locate all relevant files before the main agent performs edits, keeping both agents efficient. Explore agents terminate automatically after completing their search task, returning a structured result without leaving any persistent processes running.

features.name: Plan Agent
features.summary_ja: 実装計画を設計するエージェント
features.description_ja: タスクの実装戦略を設計。ステップバイステップ計画、重要ファイル特定、アーキテクチャトレードオフ考慮。

examples.title: Plan new feature
examples.code: Task: subagent_type=Plan で認証機能の実装計画

features.summary_en: A specialized planning sub-agent that designs step-by-step implementation strategies, identifies critical files, and considers architectural tradeoffs.
features.description_en: Designs detailed implementation strategies for complex development tasks. Produces step-by-step execution plans, identifies the critical files that will need to be created or modified, and considers architectural tradeoffs between different technical approaches. Use this agent before starting a significant new feature, a major refactor, or a complex bug fix to establish a clear, agreed-upon roadmap before any code is written, reducing the risk of costly mid-implementation pivots. Plan agents can be interrupted and their partial output used as a starting point for refinement, rather than requiring a complete plan before proceeding.

features.name: general-purpose Agent
features.summary_ja: 汎用エージェント
features.description_ja: 特化していない汎用エージェント。複雑な調査やマルチステップタスクに使える。
features.summary_en: A versatile general-purpose sub-agent for complex research and multi-step tasks spanning both reading and writing operations.
features.description_en: A non-specialized general-purpose agent capable of handling a wide variety of tasks including both read and write operations. Best for complex investigations, multi-step research tasks, and work that does not fit cleanly into the Explore (read-only search) or Plan (strategy design) specializations. Use this as a flexible parallel worker whenever you need a second agent with full capabilities rather than a specialized one. Unlike Explore, general-purpose agents can write files and execute commands, so apply the same permission consideration as you would to the main agent.

features.name: Custom Subagents
features.summary_ja: 独自サブエージェントの作成
features.description_ja: .claude/agents/<name>.md にエージェント定義を置くと、自分専用のサブエージェントが作れる。
features.summary_en: Create your own custom sub-agents by placing a Markdown definition file at .claude/agents/<name>.md with instructions and tool access.
features.description_en: Create project-specific sub-agents by placing a markdown definition file at .claude/agents/<name>.md. The file specifies the agent's system prompt, allowed tools, behavioral constraints, and any project-specific instructions. Custom agents appear alongside built-in agent types in the Task tool and are launched the same way. Use this to encode repeatable specialized workflows unique to your project, such as a dedicated database migration agent or a documentation generation agent. Custom agents defined in .claude/agents/ are project-scoped; agents defined in ~/.claude/agents/ are available globally across all projects.

features.name: Worktree Isolation
features.summary_ja: git worktreeでエージェント隔離
features.description_ja: サブエージェントを別のgit worktreeで動かしてmainと隔離。並列開発の事故防止に。
features.summary_en: Run sub-agents in separate git worktrees to completely isolate their filesystem changes from the main working tree.
features.description_en: Runs sub-agents in dedicated git worktrees, completely isolating their filesystem changes from the main working tree and from any other concurrently running agents. This prevents accidental file conflicts, merge issues, and interference when multiple agents work on the same repository in parallel. Use this for high-stakes parallel development scenarios — such as running several independent feature agents simultaneously — where the agents must not interfere with each other's in-progress changes. Worktree isolation requires that git worktrees are supported in your repository, which is standard for all modern git versions.

features.name: Skills System
features.summary_ja: 再利用可能なスキルシステム
features.description_ja: .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md でスキル定義。description のキーワードでトリガーされ、Claudeが自動的に呼び出す。
features.summary_en: Reusable instruction sets defined in SKILL.md files that Claude automatically invokes when user input matches their trigger keywords.
features.description_en: Skills are reusable instruction sets defined in .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md files within the project. Claude automatically invokes a skill when the user's input text matches keywords specified in the description frontmatter field. Use skills to encode repeatable project-specific workflows, team conventions, or specialized procedures so that Claude applies them consistently and automatically whenever a matching situation arises, without requiring users to remember the exact invocation syntax. Skills are loaded at session start and remain available for the entire session; adding a new skill file requires either restarting or running /reload-plugins.

features.name: SKILL.md Structure
features.summary_ja: スキル定義ファイルの構造
features.description_ja: frontmatter に description（トリガー文）、name、tools などを書く。本文は手順・ルール・テンプレート。
features.summary_en: A SKILL.md file starts with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, and tools, followed by a procedure instruction body.
features.description_en: A SKILL.md file contains YAML frontmatter at the top with key fields: name (the skill's identifier), description (the trigger phrase Claude matches against user input), and tools (the list of tools the skill is permitted to use). The remainder of the file is the skill body, which contains step-by-step procedure instructions, rules, templates, and any other guidance Claude should follow when the skill is invoked. The description field is most critical — write it as a specific, unambiguous trigger phrase.

features.name: Plugin Marketplace
features.summary_ja: 公式プラグインマーケット
features.description_ja: Anthropic公式や有志によるプラグイン（スキル/エージェントのバンドル）を配布・取得できるマーケット。
features.summary_en: The official Plugin Marketplace for browsing and installing skill-and-agent plugin bundles from Anthropic and community contributors.
features.description_en: A curated marketplace for distributing and obtaining plugins — reusable bundles of skills and sub-agent definitions — from Anthropic and community contributors. Browse available plugins, review their descriptions and capabilities, and install them with a single command to immediately extend Claude Code's functionality. Use the marketplace to add new capabilities quickly without writing your own skill or agent definitions from scratch when a suitable community plugin already exists. Before installing a marketplace plugin, review its SKILL.md and any agent definitions to understand what tools and permissions it requests.

features.name: Skill Triggers
features.summary_ja: スキルの自動起動トリガー
features.description_ja: SKILL.md の description に書いたキーワードが入力にマッチすると Claude が自動でスキルを呼び出す仕組み。

examples.title: Description example
examples.code: description: PRレビューしたい時に使う

features.summary_en: Claude automatically invokes a skill when user input contains trigger keywords defined in the skill's description frontmatter field.
features.description_en: The automatic invocation mechanism that makes skills activate without explicit commands. When the user's input text matches keywords or phrases defined in the description frontmatter of a SKILL.md file, Claude detects the match and automatically activates that skill. Write the description as a clear, specific natural-language trigger phrase that accurately describes the situations where the skill should activate, and avoid overly broad phrases that might trigger the skill in unrelated contexts. If a skill is triggered unexpectedly, shorten or make the description more specific; if it is never triggered, broaden the description to better match natural user phrasing.

features.name: MCP Overview
features.summary_ja: Model Context Protocol
features.description_ja: 外部ツール・APIをClaudeに統合するためのオープンプロトコル。GitHub、Slack、DBなどへの橋渡し。
features.summary_en: Model Context Protocol is an open standard that connects Claude to external tools and APIs like GitHub, Slack, and databases.
features.description_en: An open protocol for integrating external tools, APIs, and data sources directly into Claude's context. Bridges Claude to services such as GitHub for code and PR management, Slack for team communication, databases for live data queries, and any other system that provides an MCP-compatible server. Configure MCP servers in the .mcp.json file at the project root and Claude automatically connects to them at session start, making all registered tools immediately available. MCP servers can also expose resources and prompts in addition to tools, providing structured data access and reusable prompt templates to Claude.

features.name: MCP stdio Server
features.summary_ja: 標準入出力ベースのMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: プロセス起動して stdin/stdout でやりとりするMCPサーバー。最も一般的。Pythonやnpx で簡単に動かせる。
features.summary_en: The most common MCP server type, which communicates with Claude via stdin/stdout through a locally spawned child process.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates with Claude via standard input and standard output by spawning a child process on the local machine. This is the most widely used MCP server type due to its simplicity, broad language compatibility, and ease of testing. Easy to implement in Python, Node.js, or any other language and run via npx or a direct command. Defined in .mcp.json with type: stdio, a command, and optional arguments array. Best suited for local tools and scripts you control.

features.name: MCP SSE Server
features.summary_ja: Server-Sent Events型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: HTTP経由でストリーミング通信するMCPサーバー。クラウドホスト型サービスに向く。
features.summary_en: An HTTP-based MCP server type using Server-Sent Events streaming to communicate with Claude, suited for cloud-hosted remote services.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates over HTTP using the Server-Sent Events protocol to stream responses back to Claude. Well-suited for cloud-hosted services and remote APIs where you do not control or run the server process locally. Defined in .mcp.json with type: sse and a URL pointing to the SSE endpoint. Use this for third-party MCP integrations provided as remote services that need to push streamed data rather than single-shot responses. SSE servers must be running and accessible before Claude Code starts; if the server is unreachable, the connection will fail at session startup.

features.name: MCP HTTP Server
features.summary_ja: REST/HTTP型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: 標準的なHTTPでやり取りするMCPサーバー。シンプルなリクエスト/レスポンスモデル。
features.summary_en: A standard REST/HTTP MCP server type using simple request-response communication, well-suited for straightforward API integrations.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates over standard HTTP using a simple request-response model without streaming. Simpler to implement, test, and debug than SSE for services where each tool call maps cleanly to a single synchronous HTTP request. Defined in .mcp.json with type: http and a URL pointing to the HTTP endpoint. Use this for straightforward REST API integrations where you want to expose existing API endpoints as Claude tools without implementing a streaming protocol. HTTP servers must correctly set CORS headers if accessed from a browser-based Remote Control session, as browsers enforce cross-origin restrictions.

features.name: Status Line
features.summary_ja: プロンプト下部のカスタム表示
features.description_ja: git ブランチ、モデル名、コンテキスト残量など、自分の好みに合わせて統合バーを表示できる。
features.summary_en: A customizable status bar displayed below the prompt that shows git branch, active model, context usage, and other metrics.
features.description_en: A customizable integration bar displayed just below the prompt input field. Can show a variety of real-time information including the current git branch, active model name, remaining context window percentage, session name, and other configurable metrics. Configure the bar through the /statusline-setup skill to display the specific data points most relevant to your workflow. Provides at-a-glance situational awareness without needing to run separate status commands mid-task. The statusline script is evaluated on each render tick, so keep the implementation fast to avoid any perceptible delay in the prompt display.

features.name: Keybindings
features.summary_ja: キーボードショートカット
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/keybindings.json でキー割当をカスタマイズ。chord（複数キー）対応。
features.summary_en: Customize Claude Code keyboard shortcuts in ~/.claude/keybindings.json with support for both single keys and multi-key chord sequences.
features.description_en: Customize key assignments for Claude Code actions by editing the ~/.claude/keybindings.json configuration file. Supports both single-key bindings and chord bindings — multi-key sequences pressed in succession — for maximum flexibility. Use this to remap frequently used actions to keyboard shortcuts that match your muscle memory from other tools, or to add shortcuts for Claude Code actions that are not bound to any key by default. See /keybindings for a guided setup walkthrough. Test new keybindings in a fresh session after saving the file, and check for conflicts with existing terminal or OS-level shortcuts before committing.

features.name: VS Code Extension
features.summary_ja: VS Code拡張機能
features.description_ja: VS CodeにClaude Codeを統合。エディタ内でClaudeと対話、現在のファイル/選択範囲を即文脈化。
features.summary_en: Integrates Claude Code into VS Code so the current file and code selection are automatically added to Claude's context.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code directly into Visual Studio Code as an extension. Interact with Claude from a panel inside the editor, and the currently open file, open tabs, and any selected code are automatically added to the context without any manual steps. Install from the VS Code Marketplace. Provides a seamless AI-assisted coding experience without switching to a separate terminal window or copying and pasting code manually. The extension communicates with a locally running Claude Code process, so the CLI must also be installed and authenticated for the extension to function.

features.name: JetBrains Plugin
features.summary_ja: JetBrains IDE プラグイン
features.description_ja: IntelliJ / WebStorm / PyCharm などへClaude Codeを統合。VS Code版と同等。
features.summary_en: Integrates Claude Code into IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains IDEs with full feature parity to the VS Code extension.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code into the JetBrains IDE family including IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains products. Provides feature parity with the VS Code extension, including in-editor interaction and automatic context from the currently open file and selection. Install from the JetBrains Marketplace. Enables AI-assisted development for JetBrains users without requiring them to adopt a different editor or switch to a terminal. Like the VS Code extension, the JetBrains plugin requires a locally installed and authenticated Claude Code CLI to connect to for its AI capabilities.

features.name: Fast Mode
features.summary_ja: 高速応答モード
features.description_ja: /fast コマンドで Opus 4.6 ベースの高速応答に切替。Opus 4.6 でのみ利用可能。
features.summary_en: Fast Mode uses Claude Opus 4.6 for lower-latency quicker responses; toggle it on or off at any time with the /fast command.
features.description_en: Enables a faster response mode specifically for Claude Opus 4.6, reducing response latency at the cost of some reasoning depth. Toggle it on and off with the /fast command. Fast Mode is not available when using third-party API providers — those environments display 'not available' when you attempt to enable it. Enable /extra-usage first if you have reached your standard plan limit and want Fast Mode to become immediately available without waiting for a usage reset. Because Fast Mode only applies to Opus 4.6, switching to a different model with /model will automatically disable Fast Mode for that session.
